methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $54M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Inversion Space is a Series A–stage frontier-tech company in Housing & Construction founded by Justin Fiaschetti and Austin Briggs. The company has raised $54M to date, plus $71M in SpaceWERX funding. The Housing & Construction sector contains a sparse public/private comp universe of four identified private peers (ICON, Cuby Technologies, Cobod, Material Evolution) and no public comparables, limiting traditional peer-multiple triangulation.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $54M raised proxy, yielding $108M. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within Housing & Construction—only one same-sector comparable exists, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median estimation. A broader sector-agnostic peer pull would introduce material comparability noise.

The triangulated valuation range is $92M–$124M, with a median of $108M. This range reflects modest stress ($92M, assuming execution headwinds) and upside ($124M, assuming strong progression metrics). The analysis is constrained by single-method validation and sector-specific peer scarcity. Key uncertainties include technology maturation timelines, capital efficiency relative to unfunded development stages, and whether Housing & Construction comps (typically capital-intensive, infrastructure-adjacent) represent appropriate benchmarks for this stage and risk profile.
